anchor:
Senior QA Engineer – Automation (Selenium, Cypress, JUnit)  
Location: Austin, TX (Hybrid – 3 days on-site, 2 days remote) – Open to relocation & full‑time hiring only (no C2C).  
We are seeking a seasoned QA lead with 6+ years of hands‑on experience designing, implementing, and maintaining end‑to‑end automated test suites within a continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipeline. The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in Selenium WebDriver, Cypress.io, and JUnit 5, as well as proficiency in Java, TypeScript, and BDD frameworks (Cucumber, SpecFlow). Strong architectural vision for test frameworks, rigorous defect traceability, and a history of mentoring mid‑level engineers is required.  

Key Responsibilities  
• Lead the development of a unified, cross‑browser automation strategy for a multi‑product SaaS platform.  
• Define and enforce coding standards, test case ownership, and documentation practices across Agile squads.  
• Collaborate with DevOps to integrate test execution into Azure DevOps pipelines and ensure rapid feedback with resilient test data management.  
• Drive continuous improvement of test coverage metrics, performance benchmarks, and test maintenance cycles.  

Requirements  
• Minimum 5 years of QA automation experience in a fast‑paced, high‑quality enterprise environment.  
• Proven proficiency in Selenium, Cypress, JUnit 5, and associated tooling (WebDriverIO, Appium, Git, Jenkins or TeamCity).  
• Experience with Test‑Driven Development (TDD) and Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) practices.  
• Solid knowledge of RESTful services testing, UI/UX validation, and basic performance testing.  
• Demonstrated ability to mentor junior staff, perform code reviews, and lead cross‑functional troubleshooting sessions.  
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.  
• A/B testing or data‑driven test design experience is a plus.  

Preferred Certifications  
• ISTQB Advanced Test Automation Engineer (or equivalent).  
• Certified ScrumMaster (CSM) or equivalent Agile certification.  

Security Clearance: None required; firm welcomes candidates with a background in regulated environments (FinTech, Healthcare).  

Start date: Immediate. If you can bring expertise, leadership, and a passion for building robust test frameworks that scale, we want to hear from you.

positive.summary: Senior QA Lead with 6+ years designing and implementing end‑to‑end automated test suites for enterprise SaaS platforms, specializing in Selenium, Cypress, and JUnit 5. Proven track record in leading test architecture, mentoring teams, and integrating CI/CD pipelines.

work_experience.description: Built foundational automated tests for mobile and web applications using Selenium and Java. Conducted exploratory testing, created test cases, and supported CI pipeline integrations.

Developed and maintained automated test suites for e‑commerce order processing and catalog services using Selenium and JUnit 5. Implemented BDD practices with Cucumber, performed TDD, and introduced data‑driven testing for REST APIs.

Architected a unified cross‑browser automation strategy for Careem’s multi‑product SaaS platform, integrating Selenium, Cypress, and JUnit 5 into Azure DevOps pipelines. Spearheaded test framework design, enforced coding standards, and mentored a team of 6 QA engineers.

anchor:
Principal Solidity Engineer – Senior Architecture & Delivery Lead  
Seeking an accomplished blockchain engineer with 10+ years of experience designing, developing, and deploying production‐grade smart contracts on Ethereum. Must master Solidity, Vyper, and Rust; demonstrate proven expertise with Truffle, Hardhat, and Foundry toolchains, as well as familiarity with layer‑2 rollups, cross‑chain bridges, and NFT standards. Proven track record leading cross‑functional teams, mentoring junior developers, and establishing rigorous security‑audit pipelines. Experience with formal verification, static analysis, and compliance frameworks (e.g., ERC‑4337, ERC‑721, ERC‑1155).  

**Key responsibilities:**  
- Architect and scale decentralized applications (dApps) and protocol layers.  
- Champion secure coding practices and lead in‑house audit initiatives.  
- Collaborate with product, UX, and DevOps teams to deliver zero‑downtime upgrades.  

**Requirements:**  
- 10+ years in blockchain or related fields; at least 8 years in smart‑contract engineering.  
- Prior experience leading engineering squads and driving architectural decisions.  
- Deep knowledge of Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) internals, transaction lifecycle, and gas optimization.  
- Familiarity with Solidity best‑practice patterns (Design‑By‑Contract, Self‑Destruct, upgradeable proxies).  
- Strong understanding of cryptographic primitives, zero‑knowledge proofs, and on‑chain data storage.  
- Excellent communication skills and experience presenting to C‑suite stakeholders.  

**Preferred qualifications:**  
- Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science, Cryptography, or related discipline.  
- Prior startup exposure or experience in a growth‑stage blockchain company.  
- Security certifications such as Certified Blockchain Security Professional (CBS).  

**Employment terms:**  
- Full‑time, US‑based or W2/C2C acceptable.  
- Hybrid or remote work acceptable; candidate should be comfortable tri‑time zone collaboration.  
- Compensation: $200‑$250K base salary, with significant equity and performance incentives.  

If you are a seasoned Solidity leader ready to shape the next generation of blockchain infrastructure, we invite you to apply.

anchor:

- Must be based in the Eastern US time zone (NYC, DC, or Boston) but hybrid remote allowed 3x/weekday

anchor:
Senior penetration tester & IAM engineer – full‑time.  
- 8+ years of hands‑on red‑team ops, with 5+ years specifically in web & mobile application testing.  
- Mastery of OWASP ZAP v2.10+ (baseline scan & pass‑through proxy), Burp Suite Professional 2024.x (repeater, extender, session handler), and Polarion Test Management integration.  
- Proficient with Metasploit Framework v6.0+, Nmap v7.97+, Burp Intruder, and the latest Exploit Database v2.3.  
- Deep experience in operating a Kali Linux environment (rolling release) and automating scans with sqlmap 1.5+, Nikto 2.2.6, and Arachni 2.8.  
- Strong background in IAM: Okta (OIDC/SAML 2.0, SCIM 1.1, entitlements 2.0), Azure AD (Conditional Access, PIM), and PingFederate 20.x.  
- Hands‑on provisioning, deprovisioning, role‑based access control (rbac) and least‑privilege audits.  
- Comfortable scripting in Python 3.12, PowerShell 7.4, Bash, and writing custom validators for Identity Governance.  
- Experience deploying and monitoring AuthN/AuthZ micro‑services using Keycloak 24.x and Azure AD B2C.  
- Certifications: OSCP (v6), CISSP, Certified Threat Intelligence Analyst (CTIA) preferred.  
- Security clearance: NSA Level B or higher is highly desired; unclassified CVE author disclosures mandatory.  
- Must thrive in a fast‑paced startup (10–50 employees) while delivering security integrations for a SaaS platform.  
- Willing to relocate to Boston, MA or accept a hybrid model (3 days onsite, 2 remote).  
- Start date: 2026‑08‑01 or sooner, response deadline 2026‑09‑15.

anchor:
Seeking a Senior Full‑Stack Engineer (MERN + GraphQL) to join our SaaS startup as a permanent full‑time member (no C2C).  
**Location:** San Francisco metro or fully remote within the U.S. (must be able to work 9‑5 PST).  
**Start date:** 2–4 weeks from hire.  

**Technical requirements:**  
- 7+ years of hands‑on full‑stack development; minimum 5 years with a B2B SaaS product.  
- Deep experience with **React 18.2.x**, **Next.js 13** (app router), **TypeScript 5.x** and **Node.js 20.4**.  
- Proven GraphQL architecture using **Apollo Server 4.x** and **Apollo Client 4.x** (with federation).  
- Strong relational database skills: **PostgreSQL 15.3** + **Prisma 4.12** ORM.  
- Familiarity with **Redis 7.2** for caching/queueing and **ElasticSearch 8.8** for search surface.  
- CI/CD pipelines built on **GitHub Actions 5.x** + Docker 24, orchestrated via **Kubernetes 1.27** on **AWS EKS**.  
- Infrastructure as Code with **Terraform 1.6** (including modules for VPC, IAM, EKS).  
- Experience in building serverless functions (AWS Lambda v4) using **Node 20** runtime.  
- Optional but strongly preferred: **Docker 24** local dev (Compose v2), **pnpm 9.5** or Yarn 1.22, **ESLint 8.x** with Airbnb config, **Prettier 3.x**.  

**Soft skills & other criteria:**  
- Minimum 3 years leading/mentoring a small dev team (≥3 people).  
- Excellent debugging & cross‑functional communication (product, data, QA).  
- Slack‑native workflows, knowledge of **Storybook 7** for component libraries.  
- Comfortable with security practices: OWASP Top 10, Snyk scanning, Docker image signing.  

**Certifications / compliance:**  
- AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate (valid) or equivalent;  
- Familiarity with HIPAA or SOC2 regulated environments (preferred).  

**Compensation:** Competitive salary ($180‑250k base) + equity + flex benefits. Looking for a serious candidate who can hit the ground running and scale our product globally.

positive.summary: Senior Full‑Stack Engineer with 8 years of experience building B2B SaaS platforms using MERN, GraphQL, Next.js 13, and AWS.

work_experience.description: Built the core SaaS product using React, Next.js 10, TypeScript, Node.js and GraphQL, integrating Prisma with PostgreSQL, Redis for caching, and ElasticSearch for search capabilities. Implemented CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and Docker, and managed deployments on AWS EC2.

Designed serverless microservices on AWS Lambda (Node 20), utilized Terraform for VPC, IAM, and EKS, and built Docker Compose workflows for local dev. Oversaw the migration of legacy monolith to a micro‑service architecture using Next.js 13, Apollo Federation, and AWS EKS (Kubernetes 1.27). Built CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions 5.x, Docker 24, and Terraform 1.6. Introduced storybook integration for UI components and automated testing with Cypress. Managed a squad of 4 engineers and mentored junior developers.

Lead the full‑stack development of a multi‑tenant SaaS platform targeting enterprise clients. Implemented GraphQL federation, serverless functions on AWS Lambda, Docker 24 local dev, and updated CI/CD pipelines. Mentored a 5‑person dev team, driven cross‑functional collaboration with product, data, and QA. Designed secure component libraries with Storybook 7, and enforced security practices across the stack.

anchor:
Senior Frontend Engineer – React, Next.js, Tailwind (Full‑Time, Hybrid, San Francisco Bay Area)

We are seeking a **Senior Frontend Engineer** to lead the design, architecture, and delivery of modern web experiences for our enterprise‑grade digital platform. The ideal candidate will have **5+ years of professional front‑end development** experience, a deep mastery of **React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS**, and a proven track record of engineering scalable, high‑performance user interfaces that drive business outcomes.

**Key Technical Responsibilities**
- Architect and implement reusable React components and application landmarks that adhere to design system specifications
- Optimize Next.js server‑side rendering pipelines, static‑generation workflows, and incremental static regeneration for improved SEO, performance, and developer velocity
- Construct responsive, accessible layouts through Tailwind CSS, creating custom utility classes as needed
- Drive code quality by establishing unit/integration tests, enforcing ESLint/Prettier pipelines, and conducting thorough code reviews
- Mentor junior developers and collaborate cross‑functionally with Product, Design, and Backend teams to translate business requirements into robust front‑end solutions

**Desired Qualifications**
- **7+ years** resource‑based experience building enterprise‑scale web applications
- Advanced proficiency in **React (hooks, context, performance optimizations)**, **Next.js (app router, middleware, API routes)**, and large‑scale **Tailwind CSS** projects
- Solid knowledge of **TypeScript** (or strong JavaScript fundamentals), state management (e.g., Redux, Zustand, Recoil), and GraphQL / REST API integration
- Hands‑on experience with **CI/CD pipelines**, Docker, and cloud hosting platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
- Familiarity with **accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1, ARIA)** and performance audit tools (Lighthouse, WebPageTest)
- Strong communication, stakeholder management, and analytical problem‑solving skills

**Business‑Focused Attributes**
- Ability to translate complex requirements into architectural visions, breaking down into sprint‑ready stories
- Proven experience leading a front‑end team, fostering collaborative culture, and setting technical direction
- Demonstrated success in launching new features that achieve measurable KPIs (conversion, load time, user engagement)

**Additional Expectations**
- Available for **hybrid work** (3‑day on‑site, 2‑day remote) or **full‑time remote** with flexible hours (primary time zone: Pacific)
- Commits to a standard contractual relationship (no C2C preferred) with a competitive total compensation package, including performance bonuses
- Willingness to sign **Non‑Disclosure Agreements** and observe company security policies

**Benefits & Perks**
- Competitive salary ($90k–$120k base, adjusted by experience), comprehensive 401(k) matching, health & dental benefits
- Continuous learning stipend, internal tech talks, code‑quality hackathons
- Dynamic, inclusive culture with career progression pathways positioned for long‑term leadership growth

If you meet the above criteria and aspire to shape next‑generation web experiences for a technology‑driven organization, please submit your resume and portfolio portfolio PDFs to hr@ourcompany.com with the subject line “Senior Frontend Engineer – React/Next/Tailwind”.

positive.summary: Senior Frontend Engineer with 8 years of experience designing and delivering enterprise-grade web applications using React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS.

work_experience.description: Built responsive e-commerce front‑end using React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS. Integrated GraphQL APIs, managed state with Redux, implemented unit tests with Jest and React Testing Library, containerized applications with Docker, and set up CI/CD pipelines on GitHub Actions.

Led a team of 5 front‑end developers to design a reusable component library and architecture for an enterprise dashboard. Optimized Next.js server‑side rendering pipelines and incremental static regeneration. Introduced TypeScript, advanced state management with Zustand and Recoil, and enforced accessibility (WCAG 2.1, ARIA). Managed Dockerized deployments on AWS and Azure, and maintained CI/CD with GitHub Actions.

Head the front‑end architecture for Noon’s enterprise‑grade digital platform. Spearhead the migration to Next.js app router, create middleware for authentication, and develop API routes. Build custom utilities and responsive layouts with Tailwind CSS, ensuring WCAG 2.1 compliance. Champion code quality through unit/integration testing, ESLint/Prettier enforcement, and aggressive performance auditing with Lighthouse. Mentor junior engineers and guide sprint planning.

anchor: Seeking an experienced Senior Solidity Engineer (5+ years) to join our on‑prem flagship blockchain platform team. The role is full‑time, hybrid (NYC‑HQ, remote allowed for candidates with a U.S. security clearance). Must be proficient with Solidity 0.8.x, execution of audited ERC‑20, ERC‑721, and ERC‑4626 token contracts, and have hands‑on experience deploying to Ethereum mainnet (via Hardhat v2.12 or Foundry v0.4) and Layer‑2 solutions (Optimism, Arbitrum, Polygon). Desired expertise includes use of OpenZeppelin Contracts v5.0, upgradeable proxy patterns (Transparent and UUPS), and integration with Foundry test suites or Brownie. Candidates must demonstrate prior work on a multi‑signature wallet or DAO module, with demonstrable audit logs (Consensys Diligence or CertiK). Cryptographic knowledge (SM2, ECDSA, BLS12‑381) and familiarity with zk‑SNARK/zk‑STARK tooling (SnarkJS, ZoKrates) is highly valued. Strong background in TypeScript, Node.js, and a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions) that automatically runs test coverage, gas cost analysis, and deployment scripts. Please specify experience with contract upgradability via OpenZeppelin Upgrades plugin and ability to remediate reentrancy and front‑running vulnerabilities in a real world scenario (e.g., using Provable or Keep3r). Remote candidates will be considered if they can relocate within 90 days or can provide timely interface with US time zone. The successful candidate will start immediately, with possibility of contract or full‑time, no C2C.

positive.summary: Senior Solidity Engineer with 8.5 years of experience in developing, auditing, and deploying upgradeable smart contracts across Ethereum mainnet and Layer‑2 solutions, specializing in multi‑signature wallets, DAO modules, and cryptographic primitives.

work_experience.description: Led the design and implementation of upgradeable ERC‑20, ERC‑721, and ERC‑4626 token contracts using OpenZeppelin Contracts v5.0 and the Transparent proxy pattern. Conducted extensive security audits, including SM2 and ECDSA signing schemes, and produced audit reports for ConsenSys Diligence.

Architected and deployed a multi‑signature wallet and DAO governance module using UUPS upgradeable proxies. Integrated Foundry test suites and Brownie scripts to achieve >95% coverage. Implemented gas‑optimized front‑running mitigation with the Provable oracle and Keep3r for emergency execution. Managed CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions that automatically ran gas analysis, reentrancy checks, and deployment scripts to L2 networks.

Developing zk‑SNARK based oracle services with SnarkJS and ZoKrates, enabling privacy‑preserving token transfers on Ethereum. Overseeing audit trails with CertiK and maintaining compliance with U.S. security clearance requirements. Supporting remote collaboration with the NYC HQ team and preparing for relocation within 90 days.
